policy: Explicit Bowline canonical wire timestamp policy, enforced identically by the generated TS and Convex guards and the Rust wire validator (none of which delegate the accept/reject decision to a third-party date parser). Separator is 'T', 't', or a single space; dates are real calendar dates with leap-year day counts; hour 00-23, minute/second 00-59 (no leap seconds, no 24:00); fractional seconds are one or more digits at any precision; offset is 'Z'/'z' or +/-(00-23):(00-59); no trailing characters. Adversarial vectors below include known third-party parser leniencies (arbitrary separators, leap seconds) that Bowline rejects.
